title: Algal DNA Extraction Using Plant DNAzol Reagent
author: Jennifer H Wisecaver
materials: Get liquid nitrogen if starting from live cells.

steps.title: Warm the frozen centrifuge tube by...
steps.instruction: Warm the frozen centrifuge tube by cupping the area around the pellet in the palm of your hand. As soon as you see the edges of the pellet beginning to melt, add 300 µL DNAzol. Pipet up and down to dislodge pellet, and transfer to a 1.5 mL centrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add 5 µL RNAse A
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL RNAse A. Make sure the cap is closed securely, and mix well by vigorously shaking the tube for 00:00:15 .

steps.title: Incubate on the ThermoMixer at 500...
steps.instruction: Incubate on the ThermoMixer at 500 rpm, 35 °C, 20 minutes . About half way through the incubation, take the tubes out and again mix well by vigorously shaking the tube for 00:00:15 . Place back in the ThermoMixer to complete the incubation step.

steps.title: Add 300 µL chloroform to each sample
steps.instruction: Add 300 µL chloroform to each sample. Make sure the cap is closed securely, and mix well by vigorously shaking the tube for 00:00:15 . All liquid chloroform liquid waste should be ejected or decanted into the amber bottle labeled 'Phenol:Chloroform Waste' inside the fume hood. Any plasticware that comes into contact with chloroform needs to be properly disposed of in the sharps bin inside the fume hood labeled 'Mutagen/Carcinogen Sharps Do Not Autoclave' .

steps.title: Add 225 µL of 100% ethanol
steps.instruction: Add 225 µL of 100% ethanol. Invert ten times to mix.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 5000 rpm, °C, 4 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 5000 rpm, °C, 4 minutes . Orient the centrifuge tube so that cap hinge is on the outer edge of the rotor. Proper orientation of the tube helps to avoid the pelleted RNA when removing the supernatant in subsequent steps. Even if you can't see the pellet, you will know that it is located on the on the bottom corner under the hinge.

steps.title: Add 300 µL of previously prepared...
steps.instruction: Add 300 µL of previously prepared DNAzol wash and drag along the top of a tube rack to agitate the sample and dislodge the pellet.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Add 300 µL 75% ethanol wash and vortex briefly
steps.instruction: Add 300 µL 75% ethanol wash and vortex briefly. If the pellet does not dislodge at this step, that is okay.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant by taking off...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant by taking off the initial volume with a 1 mL pipette tip. Briefly spin down the tube using the little bench top spinner, and then use a 200 mL pipet tip to get the last bit of liquid. Again, even if you can't see the pellet, you will know that it is located on the on the bottom corner under the hinge. So be careful to avoid that side of the tube. Pipette away the supernatant by tipping the tube slightly towards you and pipetting the liquid from the bottom corner opposite the hinge.

steps.title: Resuspend sample in 50 µL TE Buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend sample in 50 µL TE Buffer.

steps.title: Incubate on the ThermoMixer 500 rpm, 35 °C, 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate on the ThermoMixer 500 rpm, 35 °C, 5 minutes .

steps.title: Briefly spin down the tube using...
steps.instruction: Briefly spin down the tube using the little bench top spinner.

steps.title: While the TapeStation is running, measure...
steps.instruction: While the TapeStation is running, measure the DNA concentration using the Qubit.

steps.title: Set the ThermoMixer to 35 °C
steps.instruction: Set the ThermoMixer to 35 °C

steps.title: Collect 50 mL liquid culture (approximately...
steps.instruction: Collect 50 mL liquid culture (approximately 10-100 million cells) growing exponentially in a centrifuge tube and centrifuge for 4000 x g, °C, 10 minutes .

steps.title: Wipe down your work area with 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Wipe down your work area with 70% ethanol: Chemical fume hood Benchtop Rotors on the microcentrifuge and bench top spinner Pipettes

steps.title: Pour off the supernatant into a P
steps.instruction: Pour off the supernatant into a P. pavum waste container, and flash freeze the cell pellet in liquid nitrogen.

steps.title: Immediately add the DNAzol (see step...
steps.instruction: Immediately add the DNAzol (see step 9) or store in -80 °C freezer. It's important to work quickly here as the liquid nitrogen will have lysed many cells already. Material will be highly susceptible to environmental nucleases if allowed to sit at room temperature.

steps.title: Incubate on the Hula Mixer for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate on the Hula Mixer for 00:05:00

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12000 rpm, °C, 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12000 rpm, °C, 10 minutes . Don't jostle samples when removing from the centrifuge.

steps.title: Transfer approximately 300 µL of the...
steps.instruction: Transfer approximately 300 µL of the top aqueous phase to a fresh 1.5 mL tube. Pipet slowly from the top, being careful not to disrupt the interphase.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant by taking off...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant by taking off the initial volume with a 1 mL pipette tip. Use a 200 mL pipette tip to get the last bit of liquid. This is the last supernatant and plastic waste that needs to be disposed of in the phenol:chloroform waste. All waste in the remaining steps can go in the standard lab 'look-alike' waste bin.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant by taking off...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant by taking off the initial volume with a 1 mL pipette tip. Use a 200 mL pipette tip to get the last bit of liquid. Be sure to avoid the hinge side of the tube where the sample is.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 5000 rpm, °C, 4 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 5000 rpm, °C, 4 minutes . Orient the centrifuge tube so that cap hinge is on the outer edge of the rotor.

steps.title: Air dry the DNA pellet for...
steps.instruction: Air dry the DNA pellet for 00:05:00 by placing the tube on its side inside a folded kim wipe. Do not over dry the DNA pellet as this will greatly decrease its solubility. However, also be sure that all the ethanol is removed as residual ethanol will also decrease solubility.

steps.title: Make sure all chloroform waste is...
steps.instruction: Make sure all chloroform waste is properly disposed of in the chemical fume hood. Wipe down the chemical fume hood and the benchtop with 70-75% ethanol.

steps.title: Perform cell counts using the flow cytometer
steps.instruction: Perform cell counts using the flow cytometer. Make sure you reserve time on the Accuri Teams calendar.

steps.title: Dilute 500 µL culture in 500 µL algal medium
steps.instruction: Dilute 500 µL culture in 500 µL algal medium.

steps.title: Get initial sample weight on the...
steps.instruction: Get initial sample weight on the analytical balance (the one with the enclosed hood)

steps.title: Open the Accuri C6 Plus software and open the cell_counts
steps.instruction: Open the Accuri C6 Plus software and open the cell_counts.cit template under Wisecaver/TEMPLATES directory. Count approximately 5000 'cells with chlorophyll' which should correspond to live P. parvum cells with active chloroplasts.

steps.title: Get a post count sample weight on the analytical balance
steps.instruction: Get a post count sample weight on the analytical balance.

steps.title: Calculate cell density (in cells per...
steps.instruction: Calculate cell density (in cells per mL) with the following formula: Concentration = Counts / (Weight 1 - Weight 2) * 2

steps.title: Place 100 µL of your algal...
steps.instruction: Place 100 µL of your algal sample in a 96-well glass bottom plate and check on the culture quality using the 60x objective. Take note of P. parvum cell uniformity and amount of bacterial and/or picoeukaryote contamination.

steps.title: Perform a sip clean on the flow cytometer
steps.instruction: Perform a sip clean on the flow cytometer.

steps.title: During incubation, prepare an aliquot of 100% ethanol
steps.instruction: During incubation, prepare an aliquot of 100% ethanol. Prepare 300 µL per sample.

steps.title: During incubation, prepare an ethanol and...
steps.instruction: During incubation, prepare an ethanol and DNAzol wash by mixing 1 mL DNAzol with 750 µL ethanol.

steps.title: During incubation, prepare a 75% ethanol...
steps.instruction: During incubation, prepare a 75% ethanol wash using nuclease free water. Prepare 300 µL per sample.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge at 5000 rpm, °C, 4 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 5000 rpm, °C, 4 minutes . Orient the centrifuge tube so that cap hinge is on the outer edge of the rotor.

steps.title: Assess DNA integrity using the TapeStation
steps.instruction: Assess DNA integrity using the TapeStation. Make sure you reserve time on the TapeStation Teams calendar.

steps.title: Store your sample based on on...
steps.instruction: Store your sample based on on the QC results (see below).

steps.title: If the TapeStation reports a low...
steps.instruction: If the TapeStation reports a low DNA integrity (DIN) score (< 7) and/or the DNA shows signs of banding, then the sample is too degraded to send for sequencing. If the DIN score is low, but there is no sign of banding, the sample could still be useful in the future (e.g. for PCR). Transfer the sample to a cyrovial, label with the date and the sample identifier, and place in the 'P.parvum DNA' box in the -80 freezer. Note the location of the sample in the -80 log book. If there is sign of banding, discard the sample. Start a new bulk culture and try again!

steps.title: If the concentration is less than...
steps.instruction: If the concentration is less than 20 ng/uL, then there isn't sufficient quantity for novogene whole genome sequencing. However, this sample could still be useful in the future (e.g. for PCR). Transfer the sample to a cyrovial, label with the date and the sample identifier, and place in the 'P.parvum DNA' box in the -80 freezer. Note the location of the sample in the -80 log book. Start a new bulk culture and try again!

steps.title: If the TapeStation reports a high...
steps.instruction: If the TapeStation reports a high DNA integrity (DIN) score (≥ 7) and DNA concentration ≥ 20 ng/uL, then you have successfully isolated DNA for whole genome sequencing!! Cap your sample tube tightly. Make sure each tube is labeled on the cap and vertically on the side with the sample's unique identifier*. Wrap the cap with parafilm and place sample in the 'Novogene' box in the full sized -20 freezer. Update the purple columns in the 'Prymnesium strain tracking' google sheet. *The identifier should start with a letter and be no more than 12 characters long. Write it carefully using a fine tip permanent marker as this tube will be shipped off for DNA sequencing.
